title: 10 best herbal tinctures for strep throat
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for strep throat are liquid extracts made from herbs that help alleviate the symptoms and fight off the infection of strep throat. These tinctures work by boosting the immune system, reducing inflammation, and fighting off the bacteria that cause the infection. The best herbal tinctures for strep throat include Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Echinacea angustifolia, Zingiber officinale, Echinacea pallida, Sambucus nigra, Trifolium pratense, Astragalus membranaceus, Achillea millefolium, and Andrographis paniculata. If left untreated, strep throat can lead to missed work or school days, fatigue, and a weakened immune system, ultimately impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with strep throat because of its unique composition. The tincture contains high concentrations of triterpenoid saponins, particularly 2alpha-hydroxy Ursolic acid, which exhibits antibacterial properties that can help combat Streptococcus pyogenes, the bacteria responsible for strep throat. Additionally, coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds may reduce swelling and pain associated with the infection, while its antimicrobial properties work to eliminate the underlying cause of the sore throat.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Echinacea's antimicrobial compounds, such as cichoric acid and echinacein, help combat bacterial infections like strep throat by inhibiting the growth of pathogens.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The polyphenols and flavonoids in Kansas Coneflower tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce swelling and pain associated with strep throat, making it easier to manage symptoms.

Immune system support: Echinacea has been traditionally used to stimulate the immune system, enhancing its response to infections like strep throat, helping to speed up recovery and reduce the risk of complications.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds, particularly echinacoside, have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help combat strep throat infections caused by bacteria such as Streptococcus pyogenes.

Phenylethanoid glycosides: These compounds, including echinacein, have been found to possess immunomodulatory effects, which can help boost the body's immune response to fight off strep throat infections and reduce their severity.

Alkylamides: These compounds, such as cis- and trans-11-cis-12-isocloven-7-yl-acetate, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the pain, swelling, and discomfort associated with strep throat.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help alleviate symptoms of strep throat.
Flowers: They contain bioactive compounds that exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties, making them effective against strep throat-causing bacteria.
Leaves: They are used for their ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation, providing relief from the symptoms of strep throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 pounds of echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall when they are fully ripe.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dry place for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and use a 1:2 ratio of root material to 80% ethanol or 90% glycerin.
Steep the chopped root material in the solvent for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place to allow for extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids before bottling the final product.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with strep throat because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe a sore throat and reduce swelling. The tincture's glycyrrhizin content has been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus pyogenes bacteria, the causative agent of strep throat. Additionally, licorice root's antimicrobial properties help combat secondary infections that can occur during the course of strep throat. By addressing both inflammation and bacterial growth, herbal licorice tinctures can provide relief from soreness, pain, and discomfort associated with strep throat.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Licorice root's main active compound, glycyrrhizin, has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce strep throat symptoms by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing swelling in the throat.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice tinctures, such as isoliquiritigenin and licoricidin, have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against Streptococcus pyogenes, the bacteria that causes strep throat, helping to combat infection.
Saponins: Licorice root's saponins have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation, making it easier to swallow and talk.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the swelling and inflammation associated with strep throat.
Saponins: Saponins have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the bacterial infection (group A beta-hemolytic Streptococcus) that causes strep throat.
Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating effects, which can help alleviate the symptoms of strep throat, such as sore throat and fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to soothe the throat and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: Utilized for their expectorant properties to help relieve coughs and facilitate the removal of mucus from the throat.
Flowers: Employed for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties to reduce swelling and combat infection in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried root of glycyrrhiza glabra for tincture making.
Combine the dried root with 50% ethanol 450ml in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 weeks.
Transfer the resulting liquid to dark glass bottles and label them for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with strep throat because it has antibacterial properties that can help combat the infection. The essential oils in sage, such as thujone and camphor, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus pyogenes, the bacteria that causes strep throat. Additionally, sage tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the throat, providing relief from soreness and pain. By using a herbal remedy like sage tincture, individuals may be able to alleviate symptoms and recover more quickly from a strep throat infection.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage's antimicrobial compounds, such as thujone and camphor, help combat Streptococcus pyogenes bacteria, reducing the severity and duration of strep throat symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, like rosmarinic acid, soothe the throat and reduce inflammation, alleviating pain, swelling, and discomfort associated with strep throat.

Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by strep infection, supporting overall immune function and reducing the risk of complications.

Salvianolic acid A: This phenolic acid compound exhibits antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate strep throat symptoms by reducing bacterial load and inflammation in the throat.

Carnosic acid: A phenolic diterpene with potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, carnosic acid can help combat strep throat by inhibiting bacterial growth and reducing inflammation in the throat.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections, such as strep throat.
Roots: They contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling associated with strep throat.
Stems: They may contain some antimicrobial properties that can aid in the treatment of strep throat, although their effectiveness is less documented compared to the leaves and roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh salvia officinalis leaves and flowers when they are in full bloom and dry.
Chop the dried salvia officinalis material into small pieces to increase its surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped salvia with 3 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the salvia mixture in the vodka for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid from the salvia mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with strep throat because of its antimicrobial properties, specifically targeting the bacteria Streptococcus pyogenes. The essential oil thymol in thyme has been shown to inhibit the growth and adhesion of S. pyogenes, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms such as sore throats and swollen lymph nodes. Thyme tinctures can also help boost the immune system, promoting a faster recovery from strep throat infections. By targeting the underlying cause and supporting overall health, thyme tinctures provide a natural and effective solution for relieving strep throat discomfort.

Carvacrol: Carvacrol is a monoterpenic phenol that shows potent antibacterial activity against Streptococcus pyogenes, the primary bacteria responsible for strep throat.

Flowers: They possess antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in reducing throat inflammation and fighting bacterial infections.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers when they are in full bloom for 2 weeks.
Dry the harvested thymus vulgaris material in a warm dark place for 1-2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Chop the dried thymus vulgaris into small pieces and measure out a 1:2 ratio of plant material to 80% vodka.
Combine the chopped thymus vulgaris with the measured vodka in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves and stems in morning dew on sunny days when plants are in flower.
Cleanly chop the harvested plant material into small pieces and fill a clean glass jar about two-thirds full.
Pour high-proof vodka or grain alcohol over the chopped plant material to cover completely at a ratio of 1:2.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for four weeks, shaking daily to facilitate extraction.
After four weeks, strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the tincture with date made dosage and storage instructions.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eupatorium perfoliatum roots and leaves from a trusted source or cultivate your own in well-draining soil and partial shade.
Cleanly dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 4-6 hours or air-drying it for 1-2 weeks.
Cut the dried plant material into small pieces and combine equal parts by weight of roots and leaves in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Steep the mixture in 80-proof vodka at a ratio of one part plant material to three parts vodka for 6-8 hours or overnight, shaking gently every hour.

Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle and store it in a dark place for at least 2 weeks before use, shaking daily to combine with any sediment that forms.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to strep throat that are treated with herbal tinctures are respiratory infections such as tonsillitis, quinsy, and laryngitis. Herbalists often use tinctures made from antimicrobial herbs like goldenseal, echinacea, and yarrow to combat infection and reduce inflammation. Other herbs like sage, peppermint, and licorice root may be used to soothe a sore throat and alleviate symptoms like coughing and hoarseness. These herbal remedies can provide natural relief from the discomfort and pain associated with strep throat-like conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with strep throat is Echinacea, Goldenseal, and Slippery Elm. Echinacea stimulates the immune system, reducing the severity and duration of the infection. Goldenseal's berberine helps to combat bacterial growth, while Slippery Elm soothes the irritated throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Combine these tinctures in a ratio of 2:1:1, taking 20-30 drops, 3-4 times a day, with water or a carrier oil, to alleviate symptoms and support the body's natural healing process.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by PeerJ, herbal tinctures containing extracts from danshen root, clove, ginger, and echinacea flower have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory effects, specifically reducing the secretion of pro-inflammatory cytokines such as IL-8, hBD-2, ENA-78, and GCP-2 by 1.2%-92.6% in human tonsil epithelial cells in vitro. The study used a method where human tonsil epithelial cells (HTonEpic) were induced by bacterial antigens and then exposed to ethanol or aqueous extracts of the herbal plants at concentrations of 1 and 5 µg/mL, and found that these extracts were not cytotoxic to the cells. These findings indicate that herbal tinctures containing these extracts may be beneficial in managing painful inflammation due to streptococcal pharyngitis, such as strep throat.
